title: Lifecycle Transition
description: One append-only entry in a decision record's transition log. The log is the authority: current state is derived by folding entries in sequence order, and no mutable current-state field is authoritative anywhere. Attribution lives on the transition, not on the record, so who decided what and when survives independently of the record's content.

sequence.description: Monotonic per decision record. Ordering authority is this sequence, not the timestamp: two transitions recorded in the same clock tick still have a defined order.

actor_type.description: A transition requiring accountable human review must be attributed to a principal. A system actor cannot satisfy that requirement.

reason_code.description: Controlled reason code from the governing contract's reason-code set. Free text is never an acceptable substitute for a missing code; the correct response to a missing code is a contract amendment.

successor_decision_id.description: Required for a transition to SUPERSEDED. The link is recorded by the successor's transition; the predecessor record itself is never edited.

allOf.$comment: ET-LC-003: supersession must name its successor.
ET-LC-001 / ET-LC-002: acceptance and rejection are human acts and require an attributed principal with a role.
